entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor, the crop studied for ergot susceptibility

entities.name: ERGOT
entities.description: Fungal pathogen causing ergot infection in sorghums

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: Native climate and origin of some sorghum accessions

entities.name: RWANDA
entities.description: Native climate and origin of some sorghum accessions

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: Location where sorghums were tested for ergot susceptibility

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A trait used in the design of lines for hybrid breeding

entities.name: RESISTANCE MECHANISM
entities.description: A physiologically based mechanism to resist ergot

entities.name: DISEASE PRESSURE
entities.description: Natural and artificial pressures used to test ergot susceptibility

entities.name: UNADAPTED DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Developmental failure of accessions in a new environment

entities.name: DAYLENGTH
entities.description: Environmental factor that prevented flowering in some accessions in Zimbabwe

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The process of producing flowers, which was inhibited by daylength in some accessions

entities.name: ASYNCHRONY OF STIGMA EXSERTION WITH ANTHESIS
entities.description: A trait ascribed to the susceptibility of sorghums to ergot

entities.name: HISTOLOGY
entities.description: The study of ovules to observe pollination timing

entities.name: POLLINATION BEFORE FLORET GAPING
entities.description: A physiological event that prevents ergot infection

entities.name: DISEASE ESCAPE
entities.description: The avoidance of infection due to efficient pollination rather than genetic resistance

entities.name: EFFICIENT POLLINATION
entities.description: A mechanism that leads to disease escape from ergot

entities.name: CLEISTOGAMY
entities.description: A desirable character for avoiding ergot infection in self-fertile sorghums

entities.name: GENETIC RESOURCE
entities.description: Potential value of specific accessions for further study
